methodsAttempted.query: site:ua.pt Universidade de Aveiro inteligencia artificial IA generativa ensino avaliacao estudantes politica regulamento
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl found an official AcademIA page, an AI curricular-unit page, an A3ES report PDF, and an official education news article.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ua.pt University of Aveiro generative AI artificial intelligence teaching assessment students policy guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: English search results were mostly reports, course material, and research or event PDFs rather than central AI policy guidance.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ua.pt ChatGPT Universidade de Aveiro avaliacao estudantes integridade
methodsAttempted.notes: No official central academic-integrity or student assessment rule specific to ChatGPT or generative AI was found in this search pass.

Robots.txt allowed non-api public pages and disallowed /api/ for generic crawlers. The visible page is JavaScript-rendered; the same official page content was retrieved from the University's public portal API endpoint for the rendered page.
The official portal API returned the rendered AcademIA page content, including the source title, last-modified metadata, and Portuguese article text.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery did not locate a central binding University of Aveiro AI policy. One verified official pedagogical-training page, AcademIA, supports narrow teaching-guidance claims about teacher capacity building and responsible use of generative AI in teaching and learning. Course, news, and A3ES report results were recorded but rejected for policy claims.

artifacts.verificationNotes: Official University of Aveiro page under ua.pt; rendered article text was retrieved from the University's public portal API for the same page slug.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Aveiro's AcademIA page describes a training proposal for UA teachers to understand generative AI's transformative potential and apply it responsibly in real teaching and learning contexts.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Surge, assim, o AcademIA, uma proposta formativa dirigida a docentes da UA que pretendam compreender o potencial transformador destas tecnologias, aplicando-as de forma responsavel em contextos reais de ensino e aprendizagem.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: AcademIA is a training proposal for UA teachers to understand these technologies' transformative potential and apply them responsibly in teaching and learning contexts.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Aveiro's AcademIA objectives include promoting UA teachers' capacity for pedagogical and ethical use of artificial-intelligence tools, critical reflection, practice sharing, and AI-based educational transformation projects.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.title: University of Aveiro AI Policy Source Scan
artifacts.summary: Discovery found no central binding University of Aveiro AI policy or student academic-integrity rule in official-domain searches. The staged claims are intentionally limited to the official AcademIA pedagogical-training page, which describes teacher training, ethical and responsible AI use, reflection, practice sharing, and AI-based teaching transformation projects. Course, news/commentary, and accreditation-report sources were rejected for policy claims.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The visible ua.pt page is JavaScript-rendered; rendered article content was retrieved from the University of Aveiro public portal API for the same official page slug.
No central binding AI policy was found during this crawl, so no university-wide mandatory AI-use rule is asserted.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
